meta.lastUpdated: After Round 3 · R4 Sunday · U.S. Open · 2026-06-21

prizes.label: Overall Pool Champion
prizes.desc: Most cumulative earnings, all four majors

prizes.label: Group Champion
prizes.desc: Most cumulative earnings within your group of 8

participants.name: Arif Nuraney
participants.blurbShort: Rory T17, Aaron Rai T17, Ben Griffin T29. Min Woo Lee and Harris English cut. Still leading overall by a mile.
participants.blurb: Rory McIlroy T17, Aaron Rai T17, Ben Griffin T29, Chris Gotterup T47. Min Woo Lee and Harris English cut. Aaron Rai is not the force he was at Aronimink but he adds useful supporting income. Arif leads the overall standings by such a margin that the US Open major prize is interesting but not essential.

participants.name: Matt Cavelti
participants.blurbShort: Scheffler T2, Tyrrell Hatton T29, Aaron Rai T17. Harris English and Si Woo Kim missed the cut.
participants.blurb: Scottie Scheffler at T2 is doing the heavy lifting. Tyrrell Hatton T29 adds supporting income. Aaron Rai T17, Chris Gotterup T47. Harris English and Si Woo Kim missed the cut. Aaron Rai is not the force he was at Aronimink, but he cashed nicely for the PGA and that money is locked in. KEY TO WINNING THE US OPEN PRIZE: Scheffler needs to win outright or finish second. No other path to the top three in this leg. If Scheffler wins, Cavelti's combined total from Scheffler plus Hatton, Rai and Gotterup support makes him competitive for the leg prize — but only if Clark falls, which as of Saturday evening requires an act of golf God.

participants.name: Kevin Young
participants.blurbShort: Sam Burns T6, Rory T17, Kitayama T55. Brooks Koepka and Min Woo Lee cut. Henley had a nightmare R3.
participants.blurb: Sam Burns T6, Rory McIlroy T17, Kurt Kitayama T55. Russell Henley shot 80 in R3 and fell to T68 — a genuine disaster. Brooks Koepka and Min Woo Lee cut. Burns is your weekend horse and he is positioned for a strong Sunday finish.

participants.name: Ali Pejman
participants.blurbShort: Wyndham Clark is about to make Ali Pejman very, very rich. Clark leads by 7, Ali has him on his card.
participants.blurb: Drafted Wyndham Clark back in January and has watched him do precisely nothing for two majors. Then Shinnecock happened. Clark leads by seven shots entering Sunday and is statistically one of the most dominant 54-hole leaders in U.S. Open history. Ali also has Rory McIlroy (T17), Tyrrell Hatton (T29), and Corey Conners (T13) adding solid supporting income. KEY TO WINNING: Clark doesn't blow a seven-shot lead. That's it. The last man to blow a six-shot 54-hole lead at a major was 1998 Payne Stewart — and even he only lost by one. If Clark plays anywhere near his level, Ali wins this leg of the pool going away. Cam Smith and Brooks Koepka missed the cut, which doesn't matter at all anymore.

participants.name: Adam Hill
participants.blurbShort: Rory T17, Hideki T65. Jason Day WD, Adam Scott, Berger cut. The graveyard at Shinnecock.
participants.blurb: Rory McIlroy T17, Hideki Matsuyama T65. Jason Day withdrew, Adam Scott, Daniel Berger and Russell Henley all cut or collapsed. Rory is doing everything asked of him. Without Rory going top ten on Sunday, this card generates minimal US Open income.

participants.name: Michael O'Callaghan
participants.blurbShort: Rory T17, Tyrrell Hatton T29, Hideki T65. Viktor, Si Woo, Nick Taylor all cut.
participants.blurb: Rory McIlroy T17, Tyrrell Hatton T29, Hideki Matsuyama T65. Viktor Hovland, Si Woo Kim and Nick Taylor all cut. Rory and Hatton providing steady supporting income but neither is threatening the leaderboard. Half the card is done for the week.

participants.name: Jeff Williams
participants.blurbShort: Rory T17, Conners T13, Bradley T13. Max Homa absent, Patrick Cantlay and Viktor Hovland cut.
participants.blurb: Rory McIlroy T17, Corey Conners T13, Keegan Bradley T13. Patrick Cantlay, Viktor Hovland cut. Max Homa not in the field — the wound that does not heal. Three contributing players with good Sunday positions. Rory needs to make a charge if Jeff wants to do meaningful damage.

participants.name: Jamie MacDougall
participants.blurbShort: Rory T17, Brian Harman T17. JJ Spaun, Adam Scott cut. Two horses, two ghosts.
participants.blurb: Rory McIlroy T17, Brian Harman T17. JJ Spaun and Adam Scott cut. Hideki Matsuyama T65, Marco Penge not in the field. Rory and Harman are both in position for solid Sunday payouts but neither is threatening the top ten without a remarkable round.

participants.name: Chris Cooper
participants.blurbShort: Scheffler T2, Theegala T2, Sungjae Im T17. Cooper needs Clark to fold. All of Cooper's horses need to charge.
participants.blurb: Scheffler AND Theegala at T2. That is not luck — that is a very good card. Jon Rahm and Brooks Koepka missed the cut, which hurts the overall season but does nothing to diminish this major picture. Sungjae Im is T17 adding useful supporting cash. KEY TO WINNING THE US OPEN PRIZE: Same scenario as Edstrand — Clark needs to wobble and both Scheffler and Theegala need brilliant Sundays. Cooper is actually neck-and-neck with Edstrand for third place in this leg, within statistical noise. If Scheffler wins and Theegala goes top five, Cooper makes a significant move in the overall standings regardless.

participants.name: Phil Kuzyk
participants.blurbShort: Scheffler T2, Sam Burns T6. Two horses in the top six. Brooks Koepka made a mess of things.
participants.blurb: Scottie Scheffler at T2 and Sam Burns at T6. Two of the most dangerous names on the Sunday sheet, and both are on Phil's card. Robert MacIntyre T47, Hideki Matsuyama T65 are doing very little. Brooks Koepka missed the cut badly, Max Homa not in the field. KEY TO WINNING THE US OPEN PRIZE: Scheffler needs to win, or make this a genuine contest by shooting 65 or lower on Sunday. Burns needs to charge up the leaderboard and grab a top-three finish. If both deliver and Clark stumbles — long odds, but Shinnecock Sundays have surprised before — Phil makes a run. More realistically, a Scheffler top-two and a Burns top-five gives Phil one of the better Sunday hauls in the pool.

participants.name: Tom Edstrand
participants.blurbShort: Scheffler T2, Theegala T2. Two players six back of Clark. Two shots at a miracle Sunday.
participants.blurb: The best-balanced card among the serious contenders. Scottie Scheffler and Sahith Theegala are both T2 at -1, six shots behind Clark. Justin Thomas is T29, Jordan Spieth T42, Kurt Kitayama T55. Viktor Hovland missed the cut. KEY TO WINNING THE US OPEN PRIZE: Scheffler and Theegala both need to go nuclear on Sunday and Clark needs to at least wobble. Historically: Clark leads wire-to-wire U.S. Opens. The math requires something like a combined -10 from Edstrand's two horses while Clark shoots a +3 or worse. Unlikely but not impossible — it is Shinnecock on a windy Sunday, after all. Tom Edstrand is in the best position of any non-Clark drafter to make a run.

participants.name: Rob Pettit
participants.blurbShort: Justin Rose T29, JT T29, Tyrrell Hatton T29. Patrick Reed, Adam Scott, Nick Taylor cut.
participants.blurb: Justin Rose T29, Justin Thomas T29, Tyrrell Hatton T29. Patrick Reed, Adam Scott and Nick Taylor cut. Three players at T29 going into Sunday — modest but steady earning potential. No superstar, no path to the major prize, but a quiet respectable finish.

participants.name: Jimmy Richmond
participants.blurbShort: Ludvig Aberg T47, Hideki T65, Bridgeman T55, Spieth T42. Rahm cut. Aberg shot 76 in R3.
participants.blurb: Ludvig Aberg shot 76 in R3 and dropped to T47. Jordan Spieth T42, Jacob Bridgeman T55, Hideki Matsuyama T65. Jon Rahm, Jake Knapp cut. Aberg's 76 was the wound of the round for Jimmy Richmond — a player who needed to charge instead slipped backwards. Limited Sunday upside.

participants.name: Sean Monahan
participants.blurbShort: Scheffler T2, JT T29. Jason Day WD, Jake Knapp cut. Two horses, considerable hope.
participants.blurb: Scottie Scheffler T2 doing his thing. Justin Thomas T29 is the supporting act. Jason Day withdrew, Jake Knapp missed the cut, Robert MacIntyre T47, Ryan Fox T47. Heavy reliance on Scheffler winning outright, which would generate a massive payout. JT needs to charge on Sunday to add meaningful supporting income.

participants.name: Chris Kleaman
participants.blurbShort: Justin Rose T29, Cameron Young T38, Ben Griffin T29, Russell Henley T68. Harris English cut.
participants.blurb: Justin Rose T29, Ben Griffin T29, Cameron Young T38, Jacob Bridgeman T55. Russell Henley shot 80 in R3 and fell to T68 — painful. Harris English cut. Three decent Sunday earners but the Russell Henley collapse is the defining moment of this major for Chris Kleaman.

participants.name: Phil Kinninmont
participants.blurbShort: Sam Burns T6, Fitzpatrick T10, Woodland T17, Griffin T29. Jon Rahm's 78 in R2 is the ghost of this major.
participants.blurb: Sam Burns is T6 and chasing. Matt Fitzpatrick T10, Gary Woodland T17, Ben Griffin T29, Chris Gotterup T47. Jon Rahm missed the cut with a second-round 78, which is now the defining narrative of Phil's US Open. But four contributing players heading into Sunday is a genuinely strong hand. A Burns charge on Sunday could make this the most profitable major of the season.

participants.name: Mike Richmond
participants.blurbShort: Xander T6, McNealy T17, Aberg T47. Sepp Straka and Alex Noren cut. Three live players.
participants.blurb: Xander Schauffele T6, Maverick McNealy T17, Ludvig Aberg T47. Russell Henley fell to T68. Sepp Straka and Alex Noren cut. Xander is your horse and he is six back of Clark with 18 holes to play. A low Sunday gives Mike one of the better single-card payouts of the week.

participants.name: Gareth McDonnell
participants.blurbShort: Xander T6, Justin Rose T29, Tyrrell Hatton T29. Shane Lowry cut. Three live horses.
participants.blurb: Xander Schauffele T6, Justin Rose T29, Tyrrell Hatton T29. Shane Lowry cut. Marco Penge and Aldrich Potgieter not in the field. Three players with Sunday earning potential. If Xander goes low, Gareth has a strong combined total.

participants.name: Jon Greyell
participants.blurbShort: Scheffler T2, Keegan Bradley T13. Viktor Hovland, Cantlay, Si Woo, Nick Taylor all cut.
participants.blurb: Scheffler T2 and Keegan Bradley T13 are your entire season at Shinnecock. Viktor Hovland, Patrick Cantlay, Si Woo Kim and Nick Taylor all missed the cut. Max Homa not in the field. Scheffler is carrying this card on his back and making a reasonable job of it. A Scheffler win on Sunday would make this a genuinely good US Open for Jon Greyell — even if the overall season is held together by one man.

participants.name: Jay Barre
participants.blurbShort: McNealy T17, Justin Rose T29, Cameron Young T38, Gotterup T47. Ryan Gerard cut.
participants.blurb: Maverick McNealy T17, Justin Rose T29, Cameron Young T38, Chris Gotterup T47, Jacob Bridgeman T55. Ryan Gerard cut. Four players with Sunday earning potential. McNealy is your best positioned player — and nobody had him on their radar at the start of the week.

participants.name: Sean Fairburn
participants.blurbShort: Burns T6, Fitzpatrick T10, Griffin T29. Patrick Reed and Hojgaard missed the cut.
participants.blurb: Sam Burns T6, Matt Fitzpatrick T10, Ben Griffin T29, Russell Henley collapsed to T68. Patrick Reed and Nicolai Hojgaard cut. Two of the best-placed players in the field on Sean's card. A Burns or Fitzpatrick top-three Sunday makes this one of the better US Open weeks in the pool.

participants.name: Rod Armstrong
participants.blurbShort: Sam Burns T6, Conners T13, Aberg T47. Jon Rahm cut — the Spaniard betrayed you for the last time.
participants.blurb: Sam Burns T6, Corey Conners T13, Robert MacIntyre T47, Ludvig Aberg T47, Ryan Fox T47. Jon Rahm missed the cut with a catastrophic R2 78. Burns is your genuine Sunday horse and he is in the right position to deliver a strong finish.

participants.name: Nils Edstrand
participants.blurbShort: Scheffler T2, Bradley T13, Harman T17. Bryson missed the cut again. The hex is a documented phenomenon.
participants.blurb: Scheffler T2 is doing everything asked of him. Keegan Bradley T13 is a pleasant surprise. Brian Harman T17. Bryson DeChambeau missed the cut for the third major in a row — the hex is now statistically significant. Alex Noren and Harris English both cut. KEY TO WINNING THE US OPEN PRIZE: Scheffler needs to win and Keegan Bradley needs to close to top ten. Brian Harman providing steady T17 income helps the overall total. Nils is within range of the top five in this leg if Scheffler wins outright, especially if Bradley can sneak inside the top ten on Sunday.

participants.name: Hans Edstrand
participants.blurbShort: Xander T6, Morikawa T10, Kitayama T55. Cam Smith, Alex Noren, Nick Taylor all cut. The Hans Edstrand contradiction.
participants.blurb: Xander Schauffele T6 and Collin Morikawa T10. Two legitimate top-ten Sunday earners on one card. Cam Smith, Alex Noren and Nick Taylor all missed the cut. Kurt Kitayama T55 contributes modestly. The Hans Edstrand pattern: ranked 24th overall, quietly having a very good US Open.

participants.name: David Rose
participants.blurbShort: Sam Burns T6, Ryo Hisatsune T17, Gotterup T47. Jon Rahm's R2 78 ended your tournament in round two.
participants.blurb: Sam Burns T6 and Ryo Hisatsune T17 are your surviving horses. Chris Gotterup T47. Jon Rahm cut, Nicolai Hojgaard cut, Sepp Straka cut. The anchor snapped in R2 but Burns is in the right position to deliver a genuinely profitable Sunday.

participants.name: Dino Konasewich
participants.blurbShort: McNealy T17, Justin Rose T29, Cameron Young T38. Shane Lowry and JJ Spaun cut.
participants.blurb: Maverick McNealy T17, Justin Rose T29, Cameron Young T38. Shane Lowry, JJ Spaun, Rasmus Hojgaard all cut. Three players with Sunday earning potential. McNealy is the weekend's surprise package and he is delivering.

participants.name: Kit Matkaluk
participants.blurbShort: JT T29, Cameron Young T38, Gotterup T47, Bridgeman T55. Patrick Cantlay cut, Marco Penge not in field.
participants.blurb: Justin Thomas T29, Cameron Young T38, Chris Gotterup T47, Jacob Bridgeman T55. Patrick Cantlay cut. Marco Penge not in field. JT is the horse — everyone else is filling out the scorecard. A JT charge on Sunday is the only path to a meaningful payout.

participants.name: David Puddicombe
participants.blurbShort: Jon Rahm CUT. Bryson CUT. Jason Day WD. Patrick Reed CUT. Daniel Berger CUT. Sepp Straka CUT. All six gone. A historic shutout confirmed for the third straight major.
participants.blurb: All six players are cut, withdrawn, or never in the field. Jon Rahm shot 78 in R2 and missed the cut. Bryson shot 75 and missed the cut. Jason Day withdrew before hitting a shot. Patrick Reed cut. Daniel Berger cut. Sepp Straka cut. The projected US Open earnings are exactly zero dollars. This is now confirmed as a three-major pattern. The Open Championship at Royal Birkdale cannot come soon enough. David's only consolation: Tom Puddicombe is at least playing the weekend.

participants.name: Craig Langdon
participants.blurbShort: Morikawa T10, Harman T17, Woodland T17, Aberg T47. A quietly excellent Shinnecock.
participants.blurb: Collin Morikawa T10, Brian Harman T17, Gary Woodland T17, Ludvig Aberg T47, Russell Henley T68. Daniel Berger missed the cut. Four players with Sunday earning potential. Craig Langdon is having the best major of his season at exactly the right time.

participants.name: Stephen Roberts
participants.blurbShort: Fitzpatrick T10, Morikawa T10, McNealy T17, Woodland T17. Five of six made the cut. A sensational Shinnecock.
participants.blurb: The most underrated card at the US Open. Matt Fitzpatrick T10, Collin Morikawa T10, Maverick McNealy T17, Gary Woodland T17, Ben Griffin T29. Cam Smith missed the cut. Five players in the weekend field and four inside the top 30. A clean Sunday could generate one of the top pool hauls of any participant not named Ali Pejman or James Patillo.

participants.name: Nigel Coleclough
participants.blurbShort: JT T29, Ben Griffin T29, Jordan Spieth T42, Hideki T65. JJ Spaun and Jake Knapp cut. Still 31st. In your own Cup.
participants.blurb: Justin Thomas T29, Ben Griffin T29, Jordan Spieth T42, Hideki Matsuyama T65. JJ Spaun and Jake Knapp cut. It is literally your Cup. You are 31st. JT and Griffin are both positioned for reasonable Sunday payouts but no individual is threatening the top ten.

participants.name: Hugh Cooper Jr.
participants.blurbShort: Xander T6, Fitzpatrick T10, Bhatia T17, Conners T13. Four players in the top 15. Best all-round card in the bottom half of the standings.
participants.blurb: Four players in the top 15. Xander Schauffele T6, Matt Fitzpatrick T10, Corey Conners T13, Akshay Bhatia T17. Patrick Reed and Si Woo Kim missed the cut. This is the most consistently productive card in the entire bottom half of the overall standings. No single superstar but an exceptional floor. KEY TO WINNING THE US OPEN PRIZE: Xander or Fitzpatrick needs to win or finish second. Schauffele is six back of Clark — requires a Sunday 64 and a Clark collapse. More realistically, all four of Hugh's players finish in their current positions or better, generating the highest combined Sunday payout of any four-player cluster in the pool. Hugh wins this leg if his quartet collectively outscores every other participant's six players.

participants.name: Dionne Delesalle
participants.blurbShort: Morikawa T10, Justin Rose T29. Jason Day WD, three others cut. Two workers, four ghosts.
participants.blurb: Collin Morikawa T10, Justin Rose T29. Jason Day withdrew, Adam Scott, Nicolai Hojgaard, Daniel Berger all cut. Two players carrying the entire card into Sunday. Morikawa is playing beautifully and could land inside the top five.

participants.name: Ted Reid
participants.blurbShort: Fleetwood T10, MacIntyre T47, JT T29, Spieth T42. Harris English and Patrick Reed cut.
participants.blurb: Tommy Fleetwood T10, Justin Thomas T29, Jordan Spieth T42, Robert MacIntyre T47. Harris English and Patrick Reed missed the cut. JT is the engine, Fleetwood is the steady earner, MacIntyre and Spieth provide a floor. Ted needs Fleetwood to go low on Sunday.

participants.name: Rob Snowden
participants.blurbShort: Morikawa T10, Cameron Young T38, Gary Woodland T17. Matt McCarty shot 80. Jake Knapp and Si Woo Kim cut.
participants.blurb: Collin Morikawa T10, Gary Woodland T17, Cameron Young T38. Jake Knapp, Si Woo Kim, Matt McCarty all cut. Morikawa is doing all the work — again, as he has all season. A Morikawa top-five Sunday is your best path to a meaningful payout.

participants.name: Mark Kaufmann
participants.blurbShort: Xander T6, Conners T13, Hatton T29, Harman T17. Four live horses heading into Sunday.
participants.blurb: Xander Schauffele T6, Corey Conners T13, Brian Harman T17, Tyrrell Hatton T29, Robert MacIntyre T47. Harris English missed the cut. Four players with real Sunday earning potential. No single superstar but excellent depth — the kind of card that quietly generates a large combined payout even without a winner.

participants.name: Mark Trotzuk
participants.blurbShort: Justin Rose T29, Kurt Kitayama T55. Jason Day WD, Shane Lowry, JJ Spaun, Ryan Gerard cut.
participants.blurb: Justin Rose T29, Kurt Kitayama T55. Jason Day withdrew, Shane Lowry, JJ Spaun and Ryan Gerard cut. Two of six survived the week. Rose and Kitayama need good Sundays to generate anything meaningful. The rest of the card is paying the bill at the 19th hole.

participants.name: Jamie Finkbeiner
participants.blurbShort: Xander T6, Bhatia T17, Conners T13, Cameron Young T38. Four players with weekend upside.
participants.blurb: Xander Schauffele T6, Corey Conners T13, Akshay Bhatia T17, Cameron Young T38. Sepp Straka and Alex Noren both missed the cut. Four live players including Xander six shots off the lead. If Xander goes low on Sunday and the others hold, Jamie has a strong combined payout.

participants.name: Bryson Milley
participants.blurbShort: Tommy Fleetwood T10, Morikawa T10, Bradley T13. Three quality players going into Sunday.
participants.blurb: Tommy Fleetwood T10, Collin Morikawa T10, Keegan Bradley T13. Patrick Cantlay, Cam Smith, Max Homa all cut or not in field. Three players with genuine top-ten Sunday earning potential. It is what it is — and right now, it's quietly excellent.

participants.name: JP Wright
participants.blurbShort: Tyrrell Hatton T29, Maverick McNealy T17, Woodland T17, Spieth T42. Bryson and Brooks — combined: nothing.
participants.blurb: Maverick McNealy T17, Gary Woodland T17, Tyrrell Hatton T29, Jordan Spieth T42. Bryson DeChambeau and Brooks Koepka both cut — the full LIV power package delivering nothing as promised. McNealy is your unexpected star and he has been excellent all week.

participants.name: Stu Louie
participants.blurbShort: Xander T6, Bhatia T17, Spieth T42. Brooks Koepka cut at +10. Xander is doing the work.
participants.blurb: Xander Schauffele T6, Akshay Bhatia T17, Jordan Spieth T42. Brooks Koepka missed the cut at +10 — the least elegant exit in the field. Min Woo Lee and Nicolai Hojgaard also cut. Xander is carrying this card alone at the top and needs a big Sunday.

participants.name: Aaron Watson
participants.blurbShort: Tommy Fleetwood T10, Sungjae Im T17, Nico Echavarria T62. Rahm cut, Lowry cut, Viktor cut.
participants.blurb: Tommy Fleetwood T10, Sungjae Im T17, Nico Echavarria T62. Jon Rahm cut, Shane Lowry cut, Viktor Hovland cut. Fleetwood is your headline Sunday horse. Without a Fleetwood top-five finish, the remaining players do not generate enough combined income to move Aaron up the major prize standings.

participants.name: Darren Moscovitch
participants.blurbShort: Sam Burns T6, Bhatia T17, Conners T13. Viktor Hovland and Sepp Straka cut. Your best major of the season.
participants.blurb: Sam Burns T6, Corey Conners T13, Akshay Bhatia T17, Ludvig Aberg T47. Viktor Hovland and Sepp Straka missed the cut. Three players with top-20 finishing potential on Sunday. This is quietly Darren's best major of the season and nobody is paying attention.

participants.name: Chris Morrison
participants.blurbShort: Tommy Fleetwood T10, Theegala T2. Two unexpected heroes. Patrick Reed, Adam Scott both cut.
participants.blurb: The most surprising card among the contenders. Sahith Theegala T2 is your headline act. Tommy Fleetwood T10 is your steady earner. Patrick Reed and Adam Scott missed the cut. Russell Henley collapsed with an 80 in R3. Sepp Straka cut. KEY TO WINNING THE US OPEN PRIZE: Theegala needs to win. Full stop. If Theegala wins outright, Morrison jumps into the top three for this leg. Fleetwood needs to hold his position or improve on Sunday for supporting income. This is the most scenario-dependent of any contender — everything is riding on Theegala pulling off the most unlikely thing in a field where an unlikely thing would need to happen anyway.

participants.name: James Patillo
participants.blurbShort: Wyndham Clark leads by 7. James has him. Viktor Hovland, Min Woo Lee and Shane Lowry missed the cut. This is the paradox of the season.
participants.blurb: The tournament leader is on your card and you are 45th overall. This has been the central absurdity of James Patillo's season. But the US Open pool prize? That's a different story. Clark's seven-shot lead entering Sunday puts James in second place for the major prize, trailing only Ali Pejman. Ludvig Aberg shot 76 in R3 and dropped to T47 — minimal supporting cash. Everything rides on Clark. KEY TO WINNING: Beat Ali Pejman by having Clark win and Pejman's other players (Rory, Hatton, Conners) stumble on Sunday. James needs Clark to win and needs Pejman's supporting cast to score nothing. Given Pejman has four contributing players to James's one, James likely finishes second in this leg regardless of Sunday. But what a Sunday it will be.

participants.name: Chris MacDougall
participants.blurbShort: Bhatia T17, Cameron Young T38, MacIntyre T47, Bridgeman T55. Shane Lowry and Jake Knapp cut.
participants.blurb: Akshay Bhatia T17, Cameron Young T38, Robert MacIntyre T47, Jacob Bridgeman T55. Shane Lowry and Jake Knapp cut. Four players in the field — genuine comeback story after the worst PGA Championship in the pool. Bhatia is your Sunday headline.

participants.name: Kevin Spooner
participants.blurbShort: Brian Harman T17, Hideki T65. Bryson, Patrick Reed, Min Woo Lee, Si Woo Kim all cut.
participants.blurb: Brian Harman T17, Hideki Matsuyama T65. Bryson DeChambeau, Patrick Reed, Min Woo Lee and Si Woo Kim all cut. Harman is playing well and has a legitimate shot at a top-ten Sunday. Beyond that, the card has nothing left to give.

participants.name: Geoff Stollery
participants.blurbShort: Theegala T2, Fitzpatrick T10, Bhatia T17 — five of six made the cut. Best roster survival rate in the pool.
participants.blurb: You posted the lowest Masters total in the entire Cup and have been climbing out of that hole ever since. Shinnecock is your best major. Sahith Theegala T2, Matt Fitzpatrick T10, Akshay Bhatia T17, Ryo Hisatsune T17. Ludvig Aberg shot 76 and dropped to T47. Nicolai Hojgaard missed the cut. KEY TO WINNING THE US OPEN PRIZE: Theegala needs to win or finish second. Fitzpatrick needs a top-five Sunday charge. Bhatia and Ryo Hisatsune adding supporting income. If everything goes right — Theegala wins, Fitzpatrick T3, and Clark's supporting cast (Rory, Hatton, Conners) finishes badly — Geoff could overtake Ali Pejman for the leg. A Theegala win on Sunday would make this the most dramatic US Open pool story of the week.

participants.name: Chris Gibbins
participants.blurbShort: Keegan Bradley T13, Michael Kim T17, Jordan Spieth T42. Bryson CUT, Jason Day WD, Cantlay CUT.
participants.blurb: Keegan Bradley T13, Michael Kim T17, Jordan Spieth T42. Bryson DeChambeau cut, Jason Day withdrew, Patrick Cantlay cut. Three players in the weekend field. Not ideal, but at least three prayers are still valid.

participants.name: Fraser Reid
participants.blurbShort: Fleetwood T10, Keegan Bradley T13. JJ Spaun, Patrick Cantlay, Harris English, Max Homa — all absent.
participants.blurb: Tommy Fleetwood T10 and Keegan Bradley T13. JJ Spaun, Patrick Cantlay, Harris English and Max Homa all missed the cut. Two horses going into Sunday. Not impossible to generate a decent payout, but the depth needed to challenge the leaders simply isn't there.

participants.name: Chris Ellison
participants.blurbShort: Fleetwood T10, Morikawa T10, Niemann T38. Shane Lowry and Alex Noren cut. Morikawa is your entire plot.
participants.blurb: Tommy Fleetwood T10, Collin Morikawa T10, Joaquin Niemann T38. Shane Lowry and Alex Noren missed the cut. Stephan Jaeger not in field. Two T10 players going into Sunday is a strong position. A Morikawa Sunday charge could make this the best major result of Chris Ellison's season.

participants.name: Tim Kehler
participants.blurbShort: Fitzpatrick T10, Bradley T13, Griffin T29. JJ Spaun, Alex Noren, Ryan Gerard all cut.
participants.blurb: Matt Fitzpatrick T10, Keegan Bradley T13, Ben Griffin T29. JJ Spaun, Alex Noren and Ryan Gerard all missed the cut. Three solid Sunday horses. Fitzpatrick as a top-ten finisher is the best news this card has had since the Masters.

participants.name: Theo Ong
participants.blurbShort: Fitzpatrick T10, Jacob Bridgeman T55. JJ Spaun, Jake Knapp, Si Woo Kim, Rickie Fowler all cut.
participants.blurb: Matt Fitzpatrick T10 is carrying this card single-handedly. Jacob Bridgeman T55 provides modest support. JJ Spaun, Jake Knapp, Si Woo Kim and Rickie Fowler all missed the cut. Fitzpatrick is the whole story — and he is in the right position to deliver a meaningful Sunday result.

participants.name: Russell Ball
participants.blurbShort: Maverick McNealy T17, Gotterup T47, Bridgeman T55. Bryson cut, two Hojgaards cut.
participants.blurb: Maverick McNealy T17 is the season's great surprise and he is on your card. Chris Gotterup T47, Jacob Bridgeman T55. Bryson DeChambeau cut, Nicolai and Rasmus Hojgaard both cut. McNealy is doing the work. The rest is decoration — but what decoration.

participants.name: Tom Puddicombe
participants.blurbShort: Tommy Fleetwood T10, Sungjae Im T17, JT T29. Four players alive. Your brother David has zero. Family dinner will be interesting.
participants.blurb: Tommy Fleetwood T10, Sungjae Im T17, Justin Thomas T29, Robert MacIntyre T47. Adam Scott and Sepp Straka missed the cut. Four players in the weekend field with Fleetwood genuinely in top-ten earning territory. Meanwhile your brother David has zero players remaining at Shinnecock — confirmed for the third major running.

participants.name: Chris Smiley
participants.blurbShort: Akshay Bhatia T17. Bryson cut, Brooks cut, Min Woo Lee cut, Jason Day WD. One player, 56th place.
participants.blurb: Dead last in the entire Coleclough Cup. Akshay Bhatia T17 is your sole survivor at Shinnecock. Bryson DeChambeau cut. Brooks Koepka cut at +10. Min Woo Lee cut. Jason Day withdrew. Marco Penge not in field. One player, 56th place. Bhatia is actually playing well and should deliver a reasonable Sunday payout. It just won't be enough to change the season narrative.
